subject: translucent blue-green ooze shapeshifter mimicking a hammerhead-shark-headed humanoid in a leather jacket, grinning with bared teeth while striking a pose, dripping liquid; behind it stands the actual shark-folk figure being mimicked and two more shark-folk onlookers in an industrial arena lit by fire
description: A central gelatinous, translucent blue-green creature has shaped itself into the silhouette of a hammerhead-shark-headed humanoid, complete with a dark leather jacket trimmed in stitched detail. Its body drips beads of liquid; its hands are raised in a mocking, theatrical pose and its toothy mouth is open in a wide grin. To the left, the genuine shark-folk it is impersonating crouches forward, holding what appears to be a chained tool, glowering. Behind on the right, two more shark-headed figures (one hooded, one in a vest) watch in surprise or anger. The setting is a wooden industrial structure with ramps and beams, backlit by an orange-amber blaze that fills the rear of the frame with smoke and embers. Lighting is hot rim-light from behind and cool ambient on the ooze, giving the imitator an eerie glassy translucency.
foreground: translucent ooze shapeshifter posing in mimicked shark-folk form, wearing a leather jacket, mouth open in toothy grin, hand raised
background: wooden industrial ramps and beams of a dockyard or pit-arena, with orange fire and smoke filling the rear; three shark-headed humanoid figures react to the imitator
emotion: mischievous grin, theatrical mockery; onlookers register surprise and irritation
